titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 62

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=138 status=live modified=2018-07-31 00:48:29.023074+00 | translation moa=139 status=live modified=2016-10-21 08:17:57.675434+00

p.cl: Notum sit omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie fidelibus quod Ego Agnes Comitissa de Mar

dedi et concessi . et hac mea car
ta confirmaui deo et ecclesie sancti Andree . et canoncicis ibidem deo seruientibus eccleisam sancti finini de Mig
geu in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam cum omnibus ecclesiasticis rectitudinibus ad eandem ecclesiam iuste
spectantibus . Cum terra . et cum decimis plenarie dandis  tenendum in perpetuum tam libere et quiete et ho
norifice . sicut aliqua elemosina melius . liberius . et quietius in regno Scottorum tenetur . Hac itaque elemo
sinam dedi super memoratis canonicis pro salute anime Morgun’ Comitis sponsi mei . et pro sa
lute anime mee . et pro animabus omnium antecessorum meorum et successorum .

Testibus . Ada Comitissa Matre Regis .
Alexandro de sancto Martino . Roberto de Capella . Willelmo giff’ . Willelmo capellano . Willelmo caperon . Roberto de Neuham .
Mauric’ coquo . Alano de Inuermet . edwardo . Willelmo de Mar .
Let it be known by all the faithful of Holy Mother Church that I, Agnes the Countess of Mar,

gave and granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Andrew, and to the canons serving God in that place, the church of St Finian at Migvie, in free and perpetual alms, with all ecclesiastical revenues belonging to the same church. With land, and with teinds to be fully given, to be held in perpetuity, as freely, and quietly, and honourably, as any alms are well, freely, and quietly held in the kingdom of the Scots. This alms I have therefore given to the said canons for the welfare of the soul of Earl Morgan, my spouse, and for the welfare of my own soul, and for the souls of all my ancestors and successors.

As witnesses: Countess Ada, the king’s mother; Alexander de St-Martin; Robert de Capella; William Gifford; William the chaplain; William Caperon; Robert of Newham; Maurice the cook; Alan of Invermeith; Edward [and?] William of Mar.
